title: Acronia adonis
taxonomy: Acronia adonis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Malaysia and Indonesia. The classification of Acronia adonis places it within the order Gentianales, which includes a diverse range of flowering plants. This plant is a part of the Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the madder family or coffee family. intro: Acronia adonis, also known as Adonis plant or Adonis rose, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Ranunculaceae. The plant is traditionally valued for its medicinal properties, which include potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its use in treating various health conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Acronia adonis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
